metadata.title: Ghana MSME Tax Knowledge Base
metadata.scope: MSME tax obligations, income tax, VAT, withholding tax, free zones, compliance

sources.name: Value Added Tax Act, 2013 (Act 870) — Abridged Version
Income Tax Amendment No. 2 Act, 2021 (Act 1071)
sources.note: Source document is image-only; key rates incorporated from MSME guide

metadata.disclaimer: This knowledge base is for informational purposes only. It does not replace the original Acts. Always consult a qualified tax professional or the Ghana Revenue Authority (GRA) for specific tax advice.

tax_system_overview.constitutional_basis: Article 174(1) of the 1992 Constitution of Ghana — no tax shall be imposed except by or under the authority of an Act of Parliament.
tax_system_overview.revenue_authority: Ghana Revenue Authority (GRA) — single authority administering all taxes under the Revenue Administration Act, 2016 (Act 915). Replaced the former IRS, CEPS, and VAT Service.

msme_definition.nbssi_definition: Enterprise with turnover greater than USD 200,000 and not more than USD 5,000,000 equivalent.

main_tax_obligations_for_msmes: Income Tax (personal for sole proprietors, or corporate)
Ghana Education Trust Fund Levy (GETFund Levy)

overview.basis: A person is liable to pay income tax on their chargeable income or on final withholding payments received during the year of assessment.
overview.chargeable_income: Assessable income minus allowable deductions. Each source of income is determined separately.

assessable_income.non_resident_with_permanent_establishmen: Income of the permanent establishment in Ghana.

basis_period.company_or_trust: The accounting year, or any other period approved by the Commissioner-General.

included: Salary, wages, leave pay, fees, commissions, gratuities
Benefits and allowances paid in cash or given in kind
excluded: Reimbursements of expenses incurred on behalf of the employer for proper business purposes
Dental, medical or health insurance reimbursements (if available to all full-time employees on equal terms)

paye.description: Pay As You Earn — tax deducted by the employer from the employee's income and remitted to GRA.
paye.filing_deadline: Monthly PAYE returns must be filed on or before the 15th day of the following month.
paye.withholding_obligation: The employer's obligation to withhold tax prevails over any other law or obligations.

bonus_tax.threshold: Total bonus payments up to 15% of annual basic salary
bonus_tax.excess_treatment: Excess bonus above 15% of annual basic salary is added to employment income and taxed at the graduated rate.

income_from_employment.non_resident_rate: Non-resident individuals are generally taxed at 25% on chargeable income.

included: Consideration received in respect of trading stock
Gain from realisation of capital assets and liabilities of the business
Amount derived as consideration for accepting a restriction on business capacity
Gifts received in respect of the business
Any amount effectively connected with the business
excluded: Amounts already included in employment income

included: Gains from realisation of investment assets
Consideration for accepting restrictions on investment capacity
Gifts received in respect of the investment
excluded: Amounts already included in employment or business income

exempt_amounts: Salary, allowances, facilities, pension and gratuity of the President
Income directly connected to Government or local authority activities
Income of a non-commercial public corporation
Income of a cocoa farmer from cocoa
Any other exemption granted by law

deductions.general_rule: An expense is deductible if it is wholly, exclusively and necessarily incurred in the production of business or investment income.
deductions.capital_expenses: Not deductible. A capital expense is one that secures a benefit lasting more than 12 months.
allowable_deductions: Interest on borrowed money used in the production of income
Repairs and improvements (limited to 5% of the written-down value of the relevant pool of depreciable assets per year — s.12 Act 896)
Losses on realisation of assets and liabilities
Donations/contributions to worthwhile causes approved by the Commissioner-General (s.100, Act 896)

repairs_and_improvements_limit.deductible_limit: 5% of the written-down value in the relevant pool of depreciable assets at end of year.
repairs_and_improvements_limit.excess_treatment: Excess not deductible is added to the depreciation basis of the pool.

deductions.personal_reliefs: A resident individual is entitled to deduct specified personal reliefs from assessable income to arrive at chargeable income.

fresh_graduate_deduction.description: Companies employing fresh graduates from tertiary institutions (first graduation) may deduct a percentage of their salaries and wages.
fresh_graduate_deduction.rates: Between 10% and 50% of salaries/wages paid to eligible fresh graduates, depending on the percentage of fresh graduates in the workforce.

accounting_methods.individuals: Cash basis for employment and investment income.
accounting_methods.other_persons: Either cash or accrual basis — whichever most clearly reflects income.
accounting_methods.cash_basis: Income derived when payment is received or made available; expense incurred when payment is made.
accounting_methods.accrual_basis: Income derived when receivable; expense incurred when payable.

corporate_tax_rates.category: Financial institution income from loan to farming enterprise

msme_young_entrepreneur_relief.eligibility: Entrepreneur not more than 35 years of age

post_exemption_rates.location: Other regional capitals (excluding former three Northern Regions)

msme_young_entrepreneur_relief.loss_carry_forward: Unrelieved losses may be carried forward for 5 basis periods.

business_structure_tax_treatment.sole_proprietorship: Taxed as personal income of the individual.
business_structure_tax_treatment.company: Taxed separately from its shareholders. Both the company and directors are taxed separately.
business_structure_tax_treatment.partnership: The partnership entity does NOT pay income tax. Partners are taxed as individuals on their share of partnership income.
business_structure_tax_treatment.trust: Taxed separately from beneficiaries. Distribution of a resident trust is tax-exempt in the hands of the beneficiary.

withholding_tax.overview: A withholding agent must file and pay withheld tax to GRA within 15 days after the end of the month.
withholding_tax.employer_withholding: Employer must withhold PAYE from employee income. This obligation prevails over any other law.

exemptions: Payments by individuals not in business
Interest paid to resident financial institutions

withholding_tax.supply_of_goods_services: A resident entity must withhold tax on payments to another resident person for supply of goods, works, or services under a contract.
withholding_tax.withholding_certificate: A withholding agent must issue a withholding certificate to the withholdee.
final_withholding_payments: Dividends paid by a resident company
Rent paid by a resident individual under a lease of land or building
Payments to non-resident persons (other than through a Ghanaian permanent establishment)
Payments to subcontractors under petroleum operations
Payments for examination, part-time teaching, supervising examinations, endorsement fees

thin_capitalisation.exception: Does not apply to resident financial institutions.
thin_capitalisation.impact: Interest payments are generally deductible, but the 3:1 cap limits this benefit to prevent excessive debt funding.

anti_avoidance.general_rule: Commissioner-General may re-characterise or disregard any arrangement that is fictitious, lacks substantial economic effect, or whose form does not reflect its substance (s.34, Act 896).
anti_avoidance.income_splitting: Commissioner-General may prevent artificial income splitting between persons to reduce tax.

tax_returns_and_filing.income_tax_return_deadline: Not later than 4 months after the end of the year of assessment.
what_returns_must_include: Assessable income from each employment, business, and investment
Tax paid by withholding, instalment, or assessment
Remainder of tax to be paid
attachments_required: Withholding certificates in the taxpayer's possession
Any other information required by the Commissioner-General
who_is_exempt_from_filing: Resident individual whose sole income is from employment (unless served a written notice by Commissioner-General)
Non-resident person with no chargeable income in Ghana
tax_returns_and_filing.extension_of_time: Taxpayer may apply for extension of up to 60 days from the original due date (s.30, Act 915).
tax_returns_and_filing.instalment_payments: Tax may be paid by quarterly instalments. An instalment payer must file estimated tax by the date of the first instalment.
tax_returns_and_filing.authorisation_for_entities: For entities, a duly authorised manager must sign returns. If prepared by a consultant, the consultant must also sign with relevant certifications (s.29, Act 915).
tax_returns_and_filing.change_of_accounting_year: Allowed once in the life of the company, with approval of the Commissioner-General. This alters the timing of tax payments (s.18, Act 896).

tax_planning_strategies.note: Tax planning is the forecasting of tax liability and identifying lawful ways to reduce it. It must not cross into tax evasion.

strategies.detail: Ensure all expenses wholly, exclusively, and necessarily incurred in producing income are captured and receipted (s.9, Act 896).
Donations approved by the Commissioner-General are allowable deductions. COVID-19 donations were specifically allowed (s.100, Act 896).
Companies with fresh graduates in the workforce may deduct 10–50% of their salaries as an additional deduction.
Conducting a self-audit may identify overpayments eligible for refund or set-off against other tax liabilities (ss.66–68, Act 915).

strategies.strategy: Debt funding within thin capitalisation limits
strategies.detail: During profit downturns, injecting funds as loans (within 3:1 debt-to-equity ratio) allows interest deductions.
Ensure qualifying repair and improvement expenses are properly documented to claim the 5% annual deduction limit.
Entrepreneurs under 35 in qualifying sectors benefit from 5-year tax exemption followed by reduced rates.
Pay taxes on instalment to manage cash flow while staying compliant.

overview.description: VAT is charged on the supply of goods or services in Ghana, and on the import of goods or services, other than those exempted.
overview.standard_rate: 12.5% of the value of the taxable supply or import.
overview.flat_rate_for_retailers_wholesalers: 3% — applicable to retailers and wholesalers of goods. Does NOT apply to supply of power, heat, refrigeration, or ventilation.
overview.nhil: National Health Insurance Levy — charged on the same taxable supply.
overview.getfund_levy: Ghana Education Trust Fund Levy — charged on the same taxable supply.
overview.who_pays: The taxable person making the supply, an importer of goods, or the recipient of imported services.

registration.mandatory_threshold_12_months: If taxable supplies exceed GHS 200,000 in any 12-month period, the person must register. Application must be filed within 30 days after the end of the 12 months.
registration.mandatory_threshold_3_months: If taxable supplies exceed GHS 50,000 in any 3-month period AND there are reasonable grounds to expect total supplies to exceed GHS 200,000 in 12 months.
registration.voluntary_registration: A person may voluntarily apply to register if they have a fixed place of business, keep proper accounting records, and submit reliable tax returns.
registration.compulsory_registration: Where a person required to register fails to do so, the Commissioner-General shall register them.
registration.penalty_for_non_registration: Up to two times the amount of VAT on taxable supplies from the date the person should have registered.

cancellation.voluntary_cancellation_eligibility: Only after 2 years from date of voluntary registration.
grounds_for_cancellation_by_cg: Not carrying on a taxable activity
Not required or entitled to be registered
Has not kept proper accounting records
Has not submitted regular and reliable tax returns

registration.notice_of_change: A taxable person must notify the Commissioner-General in writing within 14 days of ceasing to operate, selling, relocating, or changing ownership.

taxable_supplies.taxable_supply: A supply of goods or services made by a taxable person for consideration in the course of a taxable activity.
taxable_supplies.exempt_supply: Supplies listed in the First Schedule of Act 870. Not subject to VAT.
taxable_supplies.zero_rated_supply: Supplies listed in the Second Schedule of Act 870. Taxed at 0% — taxable person may still claim input tax credits.
taxable_supplies.exempt_import: Imports exempt under the First Schedule of Act 870 or classified as exempt in the Customs Tariff Schedule.
taxable_supplies.relief_supply: Relief from tax may be granted to persons specified under the Third Schedule of Act 870.

vat_withholding.withholding_rate: 7% of the taxable output value on standard-rated supplies.
vat_withholding.withholding_certificate: A withholding VAT credit certificate must be issued at the time of payment.
vat_withholding.filing_deadline: VAT withholding returns must be filed by the 15th day of the following month.

input_tax_deduction.general_rule: A taxable person may deduct input tax on goods and services purchased and used wholly, exclusively, and necessarily in the taxable activity.
not_allowed_for: Entertainment, restaurant meals, hotel expenses (unless the taxable person's activity IS entertainment)
Membership fees to sporting, social, or recreational clubs
input_tax_deduction.time_limit: Input tax deduction cannot be made more than once, or after 6 months from the date the deduction accrued.
input_tax_deduction.mixed_supplies_rule: Where a taxable person makes both taxable and exempt supplies: deduct only the proportion of input tax attributable to taxable supplies. If taxable supplies are less than 5% of total, no input tax deduction allowed. If taxable supplies exceed 95% of total, full input tax deduction allowed.

newly_registered_persons.goods_supply_or_import: May claim input tax for goods acquired within 4 months before registration date.
newly_registered_persons.capital_goods: May claim input tax for capital goods acquired within 6 months before registration date.

input_tax_deduction.flat_rate_scheme: Taxable persons under the 3% VAT flat rate scheme do NOT qualify for input tax deductions on goods.

tax_invoice_requirements.obligation: A taxable person must issue a tax invoice to the recipient on every taxable supply, and retain a copy.
offences: Issuing a false tax invoice or sales receipt
Failing to issue a tax invoice
tax_invoice_requirements.penalty: Fine plus imprisonment and a penalty of up to GHS 500 or three times the tax involved.

filing_and_payment.tax_return_deadline: Last working day of the month immediately following the month to which the return relates.
filing_and_payment.payment_deadline: Same date as return submission deadline.
filing_and_payment.imported_services_deadline: Within 21 days after the tax period in which the services were imported.
filing_and_payment.late_filing_penalty: GHS 500 fixed penalty plus GHS 10 per day for each day after the due date.
filing_and_payment.evasion: A person who knowingly evades tax commits an offence liable to a fine or imprisonment or both.
filing_and_payment.sealing_of_premises: Commissioner-General may obtain a court order to seal off premises of a taxpayer who fails to issue tax invoices, improperly claims refunds, fails to file returns, or fails to pay tax.

vat.tax_inclusive_pricing: All prices advertised or quoted for taxable supplies must include VAT. If excluded, it must be stated separately.

free_zones.overview: Free zones are designated areas established to promote economic development. Enterprises in free zones operate under a special fiscal and regulatory regime.
free_zones.governing_body: Free Zones Board — chaired by the Minister of Trade and Industry.
free_zones.who_can_operate: Must be incorporated under the Companies Act (Act 179) or registered under the Private Partnership Act (Act 152). Must hold a valid license from the Free Zones Board.
permitted_activities: Production of goods and services for export
Information processing, software development, computer-aided services
Financial, banking, insurance, commercial, advisory, repair and maintenance services
Any other activity relevant to the license
free_zones.restriction: Free zone enterprises shall NOT produce environmentally hazardous goods.

fiscal_incentives.income_tax_exemption: Exempt from income tax on profits for the first 10 years from commencement of operations.
fiscal_incentives.dividend_withholding_tax: Shareholders are exempt from withholding taxes on dividends arising from free zone investments.
fiscal_incentives.import_duties: Imports by free zone developers, sub-contractors, or enterprises into a free zone are exempt from direct and indirect taxes and duties.
fiscal_incentives.foreign_investment: Foreign investors may hold up to 100% of shares in a free zone enterprise. Domestic investors similarly may hold up to 100%.

sales_to_domestic_market.limit: A free zone enterprise may sell up to 30% of annual production to the national customs territory.
sales_to_domestic_market.treatment: Such sales are treated as imports and subject to applicable import duties and taxes.

guarantees_for_investors.non_expropriation: Government cannot nationalise or expropriate free zone enterprises except in the national interest and with payment of fair compensation.
guarantees_for_investors.currency_transfer: Unconditional transfer of dividends, net profits, loan servicing payments, technology transfer fees, and sale/liquidation proceeds through authorised dealer banks.
guarantees_for_investors.foreign_currency_accounts: Free zone enterprises may operate foreign currency accounts with banks in Ghana.
guarantees_for_investors.dispute_resolution: Disputes between licensees and Government are resolved through mutual discussion or arbitration (UNCITRAL rules or bilateral investment treaties).

employment.freedom_to_negotiate: Free zone enterprises may freely negotiate employment contracts, wage scales, working hours, and dismissal procedures consistent with ILO Conventions.
employment.foreign_employees: Require work and resident permits. Subject to double taxation treaty provisions — may be exempt from Ghana income tax on free zone earnings.

licensing.application: In writing to the Free Zones Board Secretariat, specifying zone and activities.
licensing.response_time: Board must respond within 28 working days.
licensing.license_transfer: No license may be transferred to another person without prior Board approval.
licensing.revocation: Board may revoke license for breach of conditions, with at least 14 working days' notice.

key_deadlines.obligation: VAT, NHIL, GETFund Levy return and payment
key_deadlines.deadline: Last working day of the following month
Within 21 days after the tax period in which services were imported
4 months after the end of the year of assessment

key_deadlines.obligation: Income tax return (extension, if applied for)
key_deadlines.deadline: Up to 60 days from original due date, if approved
15 days after the end of the month
Within 30 days of date of contract

key_deadlines.obligation: VAT notice of change in business

key_deadlines.deadline: Within 30 days after end of the 12-month or 3-month threshold period

key_rates.tax: Financial institution — farm loans income tax
Bonus tax (within 15% of annual basic salary)

key_rates.rate: 5% of written-down value of depreciable asset pool
Up to 2× the VAT on taxable supplies since registration was required

compliance_faq.question: Do I need to file an income tax return if my only income is a salary?
compliance_faq.answer: No. A resident individual whose sole income is from employment is not required to file a return, unless the Commissioner-General serves them a written notice to do so (s.125, Act 896).

compliance_faq.question: When must I register for VAT?
compliance_faq.answer: You must register if your taxable supplies exceed GHS 200,000 in any 12-month period, or if your supplies in any 3-month period exceed GHS 50,000 and there are reasonable grounds to expect supplies will exceed GHS 200,000 in 12 months. The application must be filed within 30 days of reaching the threshold.

compliance_faq.question: What happens if I don't register for VAT when required?
compliance_faq.answer: You will be treated as a taxable person from the beginning of the period when registration was required, and you will be liable to a penalty of up to 2 times the VAT you should have charged from that date.

compliance_faq.question: Can I deduct expenses for my business from my taxable income?
compliance_faq.answer: Yes, if the expenses were wholly, exclusively, and necessarily incurred in producing your business income and are not capital in nature. Common deductible expenses include interest on loans, cost of sales, bad debts, repairs (limited to 5% of asset pool value), research and development, and financial costs.

compliance_faq.question: I am a sole proprietor under 35 in agro-processing. Am I eligible for any tax relief?
compliance_faq.answer: Yes. Your business income is exempt from income tax for the first 5 years. After that, you pay a reduced rate depending on location — 15% in Accra/Tema, 12.5% in other regional capitals (excluding former 3 Northern Regions), 10% outside regional capitals, or 5% in the former 3 Northern Regions.

compliance_faq.question: My bonus this year is 20% of my annual basic salary. How is it taxed?
compliance_faq.answer: The first 15% of your annual basic salary paid as bonus is taxed at a flat rate of 5%. The remaining 5% (the excess above the 15% threshold) is added to your employment income and taxed at the graduated rate.

compliance_faq.question: Is a partnership taxed as an entity?
compliance_faq.answer: No. A partnership itself does not pay income tax. Each partner is taxed individually on their share of the partnership income.

compliance_faq.question: How does VAT withholding work for my business?
compliance_faq.answer: If your customer is a VAT Withholding Agent appointed by the Commissioner-General, they will withhold 7% of the taxable output value (the VAT component) when paying you and remit it directly to GRA. They must issue you a withholding VAT credit certificate.

compliance_faq.question: Can I get a refund if I overpay VAT?
compliance_faq.answer: Yes. If your deductible input tax exceeds your output tax in a tax period, the excess is treated as a credit or refund under Section 50 of Act 870.

compliance_faq.question: What are the benefits of operating in a Free Zone?
compliance_faq.answer: Free zone enterprises enjoy 10 years of income tax exemption, followed by a maximum rate of 8%. Shareholders are exempt from withholding tax on dividends. Imports into the free zone are exempt from all direct and indirect taxes and duties. Foreign and domestic investors may each hold up to 100% of shares.

compliance_faq.question: How much of a free zone enterprise's output can be sold locally?
compliance_faq.answer: Up to 30% of annual production may be sold to the national customs territory, but such sales are treated as imports and subject to applicable customs duties and taxes.

compliance_faq.question: What is the penalty for late VAT filing?
compliance_faq.answer: GHS 500 fixed penalty plus GHS 10 for each day the return remains unfiled after the due date.

compliance_faq.question: Can GRA seal my business premises?
compliance_faq.answer: Yes. The Commissioner-General may obtain a court order to seal premises if you fail to issue tax invoices, improperly claim refunds, fail to file tax returns, or fail to pay tax when due.

compliance_faq.question: Is my donation to a charity deductible?
compliance_faq.answer: Yes, if the cause is a worthwhile cause approved by the Commissioner-General. Qualifying worthwhile causes include approved charitable organisations, scholarship schemes, rural/urban development, and sports development.

financial_institutions.note: Banks and financial institutions are excluded from the thin capitalisation rule. They compute VAT input tax deductions only on amounts directly linked to taxable supplies.

petroleum_operations.note: Petroleum operations are taxed separately per petroleum right. Specific deduction rules apply. Tax is payable 30 days after each quarter. Withholding tax applies to dividends between resident petroleum companies and to expatriate employee income.

mining_operations.note: Mineral operations are taxed separately per mineral right. Similar regime to petroleum. Approved rehabilitation funds are exempt from tax.

insurance.general_insurance: Income computed separately from other activities. Income includes premiums received, reinsurance proceeds, and unexpired risk reserves.
insurance.life_insurance: Income computed separately. Life insurance gains paid by a resident insurer to the insured are exempt from tax.

retirement_funds.note: Retirement contributions are exempt from tax. Retirement payments from a resident fund are excluded from the beneficiary's income.

charitable_organisations.note: Income of approved charitable organisations is exempt from tax, EXCEPT income from business activities.

taxpayer_rights_and_obligations.rights: GRA publishes taxpayer rights and obligations guidance. Taxpayers have the right to appeal assessments.

tax_avoidance_vs_evasion.avoidance: Legal arrangement of affairs to reduce tax liability. Permitted, subject to the general anti-avoidance rule.
tax_avoidance_vs_evasion.evasion: Knowingly evading tax payable. A criminal offence liable to fines and/or imprisonment.

taxpayer_rights_and_obligations.assessment_correction: The Commissioner-General may make an assessment if there is reasonable belief tax may not be paid or a return is incorrect.
taxpayer_rights_and_obligations.self_assessment: Taxpayers are expected to self-assess and file accurate returns.
taxpayer_rights_and_obligations.registration_obligation: All persons earning chargeable income, or making taxable supplies above the VAT threshold, must register with GRA.

chatbot_intents.summary: Explain income tax basis, chargeable vs assessable income, resident vs non-resident treatment.
Explain PAYE, what counts as employment income, bonus tax at 5%/15% threshold, overtime treatment.
Explain what counts as business income, what expenses are deductible, and limits on deductions.

triggers: do I need to register for vat
chatbot_intents.summary: Explain GHS 200,000 threshold, 30-day application rule, penalties for non-registration.
Last working day of following month for standard VAT. 21 days for imported services. GHS 500 + GHS 10/day penalty.

triggers: how much tax does a company pay
chatbot_intents.summary: 25% standard. 22% hotels. 8% non-traditional exports. Special reduced rates for young entrepreneurs.
5-year exemption for entrepreneurs under 35 in qualifying sectors, then reduced rates by location.
10-year income tax exemption, max 8% after, exempt from import duties, no dividend withholding tax.
Explain withholding obligations for employers, investment income, supply of goods/services. 15-day filing rule.
Summarise all key deadlines: PAYE 15th, VAT last working day, income tax 4 months after year-end.

triggers: what happens if I don't file
chatbot_intents.summary: Describe penalties for late filing, non-registration, evasion, failure to issue invoices.
Partnership itself doesn't pay income tax. Partners taxed individually on their share.
Explain deductible input tax rules, exclusions (entertainment, clubs), 6-month time limit, mixed supply apportionment.

fallback_responses.unknown_tax_question: I can help with income tax, VAT, PAYE, withholding tax, free zone incentives, corporate tax rates, filing deadlines, and MSME tax planning in Ghana. Please ask your question in one of those areas.
fallback_responses.needs_clarification: I need a bit more detail to answer accurately. Could you clarify whether your question is about income tax, VAT, PAYE, withholding tax, or something else?
fallback_responses.professional_advice_disclaimer: This information is for general guidance only and is based on Ghanaian tax legislation as summarised in our knowledge base. For specific advice about your situation, please consult a qualified tax professional or contact the Ghana Revenue Authority (GRA) directly.
